purpose: A small, source-linked public evidence desk. Records are selected for a specific documented change, not because a name or topic appeared frequently in news coverage.

method.selection: A homepage record must identify a concrete dated event, a source type, a concise factual record, a bounded system context, and an explicit statement of what the record does not establish.
method.publication_gate: Automated feeds can surface candidates, but they cannot publish an Evidence Desk record. A record is added only after its source, wording, attribution, date, and limits are reviewed.
method.attribution: TriWei links to the original source and writes a short independent summary. It does not reproduce article bodies, imply endorsement, or claim ownership of third-party reporting, research, names, or marks.
method.automation: AI tools may assist research, structuring, validation, and drafting. AI assistance must be disclosed, and no live model decides truth, importance, or legal or ethical merit when the page loads.
method.non_claim: Inclusion is not endorsement, ranking, prediction, investment advice, legal advice, or a declaration that a disputed matter has been finally resolved.

evidence_classes.label: Developer announcement
evidence_classes.meaning: A developer states that an event occurred or commits to a future action. The statement is attributed to the developer and is not treated as independent verification.

evidence_classes.label: Released artifact
evidence_classes.meaning: A cited repository, checkpoint, paper, filing, or other public artifact was available when TriWei checked the record.

evidence_classes.label: Provider-attributed claim
evidence_classes.meaning: A named organization publishes an allegation or attribution. TriWei records who made the claim and does not present it as an independent adjudication.

evidence_classes.label: Public law or regulation
evidence_classes.meaning: A government or public institution documents a legal or regulatory event. TriWei does not provide individualized legal advice.

evidence_classes.label: Company project disclosure
evidence_classes.meaning: A company describes a physical project, investment, status, or target date. Future schedules, output, and economics remain contingent.

records.title: Moonshot launched Kimi K3 and announced a later weights release
records.record: Moonshot launched Kimi K3 through its products and API and said full model weights would be released by July 27, 2026.
records.context: The record separates product availability from public checkpoint availability. Those are related but different forms of access.
records.limits: This record does not establish that the weights were released, that independent evaluators reproduced performance claims, or that the model is practical to deploy.
records.status: Release announced; checkpoint not verified in this ledger

records.title: Anthropic published an attribution concerning large-scale output extraction
records.record: Anthropic said it linked more than 16 million exchanges across roughly 24,000 accounts to DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax.
records.context: The record documents Anthropic's published attribution and the scale it reported. It does not convert that attribution into an independent finding.
records.limits: This record does not establish a judicial ruling, regulator finding, admission, or independently adjudicated account of the alleged activity.
records.status: Attributed claim; independently unresolved in this ledger

records.title: Micron began vertical construction on its planned New York DRAM campus
records.record: Micron reported a January 2026 groundbreaking for a planned leading-edge DRAM campus in Clay, New York, with up to four fabs contemplated over more than 20 years.
records.context: The record shows that memory capacity depends on long physical build, qualification, and yield-ramp cycles rather than software demand alone.
records.limits: This record does not guarantee the full investment, construction sequence, production date, output volume, customer demand, or achieved yield.
records.status: Vertical construction reported; production remains long-horizon

records.title: Moonshot published Kimi K2 base and instruct checkpoints
records.record: Moonshot published Kimi K2 base and instruct checkpoints for a mixture-of-experts model described as one trillion total parameters with 32 billion active parameters.
records.context: A public checkpoint is stronger evidence of access than a future release commitment, while practical use still depends on license terms, hardware, software, and independent testing.
records.limits: This record does not establish universal benchmark superiority, low deployment cost, complete training-data provenance, or fitness for a particular use.
records.source_label: Kimi K2 repository and technical report

records.title: The European Union AI Act entered into force
records.record: The European Commission records that the EU AI Act entered into force on August 1, 2024, establishing a risk-based regulatory framework that includes rules for general-purpose AI.
records.context: The event moved part of the AI-accountability debate from voluntary practice into a binding legal framework with staged obligations.
records.limits: This record does not assess a particular organization's compliance, predict enforcement outcomes, or provide legal advice about any person or system.
records.status: Law in force; application and enforcement are staged

threads.title: Model access and provenance
threads.status: Released artifacts, future commitments, disclosed distillation, and provider-attributed claims remain separate evidence classes.

threads.title: Physical AI supply chain
threads.status: Materials, wafers, fab tools, accelerators, HBM, packaging, systems, and policy gates are tracked as separate stages.

threads.title: Accountability and law
threads.status: Research critiques, product adoption, regulation, and limiting evidence are connected without declaring a final verdict.

threads.title: AI for science
threads.status: Documented scientific milestones are paired with evidence about experimental, interpretive, and domain-specific limits.

standards.title: Facts remain source-linked
standards.text: A factual record identifies the source, source role, date, and last review. A link is not proof by itself; it makes the basis inspectable.

standards.title: Claims remain attributed
standards.text: Allegations, forecasts, performance claims, and future commitments are presented as statements by the named source unless independent evidence supports more.

standards.title: TriWei context is labeled
standards.text: Explanatory connections written by TriWei are marked as system context. They are not represented as quotations, source conclusions, or neutral facts.

standards.title: Unknowns remain visible
standards.text: Every record states what it does not establish. Missing evidence is not filled with confident language, popularity counts, or automated inference.

standards.title: Attribution is preserved
standards.text: TriWei links outward and uses concise original summaries. It does not copy article bodies or imply ownership of third-party research, reporting, names, or marks.

standards.title: Corrections are part of the record
standards.text: Material corrections, clarifications, source changes, and expired records must be documented rather than silently rewritten.
